Turn 101 ‚ÄĒ the dyadic determinant theorem is no longer a black box

Turn 100 isolated the sole remaining input as the local zero-count formula (100.1).The candidate remains unchanged, with 40491355905 denoting the verifier residue of the genuine profinite idempotent ŌČ2.sigma,tau,x0,x1 sigma2 = sigma^40491355905 u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905 u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905 d0 = u0*x0^-1 z0 = x0^sigma2 c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0 x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0

Let F=Q2, let V be a nontrivial self-dual simple tame F2[GF]-module of dimension d, and let q be a GF -invariant nonsingular quadratic form on V. WriteQloc:H1 (F,V)‚ü∂H2(F,F2)‚ČÉF2 for the connecting quadratic form of the central extension defined by q.

I prove directly that

#Qloc‚ąí1(0)=‚é© ‚é®‚éß2 d‚ąí1‚ąí2 d/2‚ąí1,2 d‚ąí1+2 d/2‚ąí1,

V¬†unramified,V¬†ramified.(101.1)The exact dyadic filtration Choose a finite tame Galois splitting field K/F, put H=Gal(K/F), and lete=vK(2)be its odd ramification index. SetM=K√ó/K√ó2,Ui=im(1+pKi‚ÜíM).

The square expansion (1+bŌÄr)2=1+b2ŌÄ2r+2bŌÄr gives the complete filtration:M/U1‚ČÉF2,U2e+1=0,U2e‚ČÉF2,(101.2)and, for 1‚Č§j<2e,grjM=Uj/Uj+1‚ČÉ{kK,0,j¬†odd,j¬†even.(101.3)

For odd j, tame inertia acts on grj M through the j-th power of the fundamental character. Since e is odd, the middle layer j=e has trivial inertia. The valuation line and the terminal layerU2e also have trivial inertia.

The leading-term Hilbert-symbol formula givesUi‚ä•=U2e‚ąíi+1,(101.4)and therefore perfect H-equivariant pairingsgrj

M√ógr2e‚ąíj

M‚ü∂F2 (j¬†odd).(101.5)

Finally,‚ąí1=1‚ąí2‚ąąUe.(101.6)These indices are the whole mechanism: every nontrivial ramified layer below depth e is paired with exactly one layer above depth e.

Ramified V: the deep half is a canonical Lagrangian Take H to be the faithful tame image on V. Let I=‚ü®T‚ü© have order e, and write f=orde (2)=2a r,r¬†odd.

If S denotes Frobenius, then Sf is central in H. Its action lies in the multiplicative group of the finite field EndH (V), hence has odd order. Faithfulness therefore implies that the Sylow 2-subgroup of H has order 2a.Over a splitting field, this Sylow subgroup freely cycles each 2a -block of the inertia-weight orbit. Consequently V restricts freely to a Sylow 2-subgroup and is projective as an F2 [H]-module. The same holds for V‚ą®.Inflation‚Äďrestriction and Kummer theory therefore give an exact identificationX:=H1 (F,V)‚ČÉHomH(V‚ą®,M),(101.7)and HomH(V‚ą®,‚ąí) is exact on the unit filtration.

Because V is ramified, no map from V‚ą®lands in the valuation line, the middle layer j=e, or the terminal layer: all three have trivial inertia. Hence dimX=1‚Č§j<2e j¬†odd,jÓÄ†=e‚ąĎdimHomH(V‚ą®,grjM).(101.8)

Define the deep subspaceX+=HomH(V‚ą®,Ue+1).(101.9)The pairings (101.5), self-duality V‚ČÉV‚ą®, and projective-injective simplicity of V identify the multiplicity at depth j with that at depth 2e‚ąíj. Exactly one depth in every pair lies above e. ThereforedimX+=21 dimX.(101.10)

Local Euler characteristic and Tate duality give dimX=d:indeed H0 (F,V)=H2 (F,V)=0 and F=Q2. ThusdimX+=d/2.(101.11)

It remains to prove that Qloc vanishes on X+, not merely that its polarization does.

Since V is projective, choose an H-split embeddingV‚ü∂iF2[H]N‚ü∂p

V,pi=1,and extend q toq=q‚ąėp. Under Shapiro, a class in i‚ąó(X+) is represented by scalar Kummer classesőĪ1,‚Ä¶,őĪN‚ąąUe+1.

Every H-orbit monomial of the quadratic functionq evaluates as a corestriction of terms of the formőĪr ‚Ć£hőĪsorőĪr2.

All conjugates hőĪs remain inUe+1. By (101.4),Ue+1‚äÜUe=Ue+1‚ä•,so every cross-term vanishes. Moreover,őĪr2=őĪr ‚Ć£(‚ąí1)=0 by (101.6) and the same orthogonality. ThereforeQlocX+=0.(101.12)

The polarization of Qloc is the perfect local Tate pairing transported through the nonsingular polar form of q. Hence Qloc is nonsingular. Equations (101.11)‚Äď(101.12) exhibit a half-dimensional totally singular subspace, so the ramified form is hyperbolic:

Arf(Qloc)=0.(101.13)Thus

#Qloc‚ąí1 (0)=2 d‚ąí1+2 d/2‚ąí1.(101.14)This is uniform over every ramified simple tame module: the depth reflection j‚ÜĒ2e‚ąíj, rather than any individual inertia character, supplies the Lagrangian.

Unramified V: the norm‚Äďtrace form is minus type Now assume inertia acts trivially. The faithful image H is cyclic of odd order. Take K/F to be the corresponding unramified extension. Here e=1, so the square-class filtration isM/U1‚ČÉF2,U1/U2‚ČÉkK,U2‚ČÉF2.(101.15)The outer two lines are H-trivial. Since V is nontrivial,H1 (F,V)‚ČÉHomH(V‚ą®,kK).(101.16)

A normal basis gives kK‚ČÉF2 [H]. Put

D=EndH(V).Because H is cyclic, V is one-dimensional over the finite field D, and (101.16) is also one-dimensional over D. Self-duality induces a nontrivial involution x‚üľx‚ąó on D, whose fixed field is D0. Write‚ą£D0 ‚ą£=2m,‚ą£D‚ą£=22m,d=2m.Naturality of the local Tate pairing with respect to D makes the polarization of Qloc a nonsingular D/D0 -Hermitian trace form. Hence, after choosing a D-basis, it isBa (x,y)=TrD0/F2 (a(xy‚ąó+x‚ąóy))(101.17)for some a‚ąąD0√ó.

The quadratic formqa (x)=TrD0/F2(axx‚ąó)(101.18)has polarization Ba. Both Qloc and qa are invariant under the norm-one group

U={u‚ąąD√ó:uu‚ąó=1},because U consists of coefficient-module isometries of q. Their difference is therefore an U-invariant linear form.There is no nonzero such linear form. Writing a linear form as ‚Ąď(x)=TrD/F2 (bx),invariance under any uÓÄ†=1 in U gives b(u‚ąí1)=0,and hence b=0. ThusQloc=qa.(101.19)

To count its zeros, the norm mapND/D0:D√ó‚ÜíD0√óhas fibers of size 2m+1. The trace equationTrD0/F2 (ay)=0 has 2 m‚ąí1‚ąí1 nonzero solutions y‚ąąD0√ó. Therefore

#Qloc‚ąí1(0)=1+(2m+1)(2 m‚ąí1‚ąí1)=2 2m‚ąí1‚ąí2 m‚ąí1=2 d‚ąí1‚ąí2 d/2‚ąí1.(101.20)Hence the unramified form has Arf invariant 1.

Closure of the Turn-100 theorem Equations (101.14) and (101.20) prove exactly the dyadic determinant theorem (100.1). It is no longer an imported local-field proposition: it follows from the explicit square-class filtration, depth-reflection Hilbert pairing, the identity a2=a‚Ć£(‚ąí1), and the unramified norm map.

Turn 100 already proves that these two zero counts are the only local data used by the fixed-frame induction. Therefore, within that proof framework, the unchanged A2 relation now has equal surjection counts to GQ2 for every finite target; no finite-group classification theorem or packaged determinant theorem remains.

ESTABLISHED: Proved the full dyadic determinant zero-count theorem directly. Ramified modules have a canonical half-dimensional totally singular deep-unit subspace arising uniformly from the reflection j‚ÜĒ2e‚ąíj, so their local form is hyperbolic. Unramified modules yield the explicit norm‚Äďtrace form and are minus type. This removes the sole mathematical input left in Turn 100.

GAP: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The long proof stack still needs consolidation into a single independently checkable manuscript, especially the Shapiro/corestriction realization of the deep-unit quadratic restriction.

NEXT: Perform a dependency-and-sign audit of the now unconditional fixed-frame theorem, concentrating on the two interfaces where errors would propagate globally: the deep-unit Shapiro calculation above and the Turn-100 central-cover transform.[BREAKTHROUGH]
